_comment: parser-worker — CSC demo parser → fragg-3.0 stats ingest. Import this file into the Pterodactyl panel: Nests → (your nest) → Import Egg.
name: Parser Worker
author: admin@example.com
description: Polls the CSC core GraphQL API for completed matches in a configured season, downloads each demo archive (.zip or .7z, sniffed by magic bytes), parses it via github.com/ethsmith/eco-rating, and posts the resulting player-stats to the fragg-3.0 stats API. Skips matches where either roster belongs to a test franchise (TFR / Test Franchise / TestHomeTeam etc.). Idempotent: re-runs are safe and skip already-ingested matches.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# parser-worker Pterodactyl installer.
#
# Runs inside ghcr.io/parkervcp/installers:debian. All build work happens
# under /mnt/server/.build because /tmp is a small tmpfs in Pterodactyl
# install containers (typically 64 MB) and can't hold the Go toolchain.
# Following the convention used by parkervcp/eggs: stage everything on the
# server's persistent volume, then `rm -rf` the build dir at the end so
# only the binary remains.

set -euo pipefail

GIT_ADDRESS="${GIT_ADDRESS:-}"
GIT_BRANCH="${GIT_BRANCH:-main}"
GO_VERSION="${GO_VERSION:-1.25.0}"

BUILD_DIR=/mnt/server/.build
GO_ROOT="${BUILD_DIR}/go"
GO_TGZ="${BUILD_DIR}/go.tar.gz"
SRC_DIR="${BUILD_DIR}/src"

echo "================================================================"
echo " parser-worker installer"
echo "   GIT_ADDRESS = ${GIT_ADDRESS}"
echo "   GIT_BRANCH  = ${GIT_BRANCH}"
echo "   GO_VERSION  = ${GO_VERSION}"
echo "   BUILD_DIR   = ${BUILD_DIR}  (under the server's 50 GB volume)"
echo "================================================================"

if [ -z "${GIT_ADDRESS}" ] || [ "${GIT_ADDRESS}" = "https://github.com/your-user/parser-worker.git" ]; then
  echo "FATAL: GIT_ADDRESS is empty or still the placeholder. Set it in the panel and reinstall." >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "${BUILD_DIR}"
cd /mnt/server

echo ""
echo "[step 1/6] Verifying tools in the install container"
for t in bash curl git tar; do
  if command -v "$t" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf '   %-6s -> %s\n' "$t" "$(command -v $t)"
  else
    echo "FATAL: required tool '$t' not found on PATH" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
done
echo "   disk free under /mnt/server:"
df -h /mnt/server | sed 's/^/      /'

echo ""
echo "[step 2/6] Downloading Go ${GO_VERSION} (~80 MB) to ${GO_TGZ}"
rm -f "${GO_TGZ}"
curl --connect-timeout 15 --max-time 600 --retry 3 -fSL --progress-bar \
  "https://go.dev/dl/go${GO_VERSION}.linux-amd64.tar.gz" -o "${GO_TGZ}"
ls -lh "${GO_TGZ}"

echo ""
echo "[step 3/6] Extracting Go toolchain to ${GO_ROOT}"
rm -rf "${GO_ROOT}"
tar -C "${BUILD_DIR}" -xzf "${GO_TGZ}"
rm -f "${GO_TGZ}"
export PATH="${GO_ROOT}/bin:${PATH}"
go version

echo ""
echo "[step 4/6] Cloning ${GIT_ADDRESS} (branch ${GIT_BRANCH}) into ${SRC_DIR}"
rm -rf "${SRC_DIR}"
# 5-min timeout so an unreachable git host fails fast instead of hanging.
timeout 300 git clone --depth=1 --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_ADDRESS}" "${SRC_DIR}"
ls -la "${SRC_DIR}"
if [ ! -d "${SRC_DIR}/cmd/worker" ]; then
  echo "FATAL: ${SRC_DIR}/cmd/worker missing in cloned repo (wrong branch / wrong repo?)" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "[step 5/6] Building parser-worker (first build downloads ~300 MB of Go modules; can take 2-5 min)"
cd "${SRC_DIR}"
export CGO_ENABLED=0
export GOTOOLCHAIN=auto
export GOMODCACHE="${BUILD_DIR}/gomodcache"
export GOCACHE="${BUILD_DIR}/gocache"
# GOTMPDIR + TMPDIR: keep the compiler's intermediate object files
# (\$WORK) off /tmp, which is a tiny tmpfs in the install container.
export GOTMPDIR="${BUILD_DIR}/gotmp"
export TMPDIR="${BUILD_DIR}/tmp"
mkdir -p "${GOTMPDIR}" "${TMPDIR}"
go build -trimpath -ldflags="-s -w" -o /mnt/server/parser-worker ./cmd/worker

echo ""
echo "[step 6/6] Cleanup + verification"
cd /mnt/server
rm -rf "${BUILD_DIR}"
chmod +x /mnt/server/parser-worker
if [ ! -x /mnt/server/parser-worker ]; then
  echo "FATAL: /mnt/server/parser-worker missing or not executable after build" >&2
  ls -la /mnt/server
  exit 1
fi
ls -lh /mnt/server/parser-worker
echo ""
echo "================================================================"
echo " Install complete. Press Start in the panel."
echo "================================================================"

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: Public Git URL to clone parser-worker from. e.g. https://github.com/your-user/parser-worker.git

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Branch (or tag) of parser-worker to build from.

variables.name: Go Toolchain Version
variables.description: Go version downloaded during install (the eco-rating parser requires Go >= 1.25). Format: 1.25.0

variables.name: CSC Season
variables.description: CSC season number to poll for completed matches. Required.

variables.name: Stats API URL
variables.description: Base URL of the fragg-3.0 stats API (no trailing slash). Required.

variables.name: Stats API Key
variables.description: Bearer token matching the stats API's API_KEY env var. Sent as Authorization: Bearer <key>. Required and sensitive.

variables.name: Match Type
variables.description: Which CSC match type to fetch. Usually Regulation; Combine for combine games; Scrim for scrims.

variables.name: CSC GraphQL URL
variables.description: Override the CSC core GraphQL endpoint. Default works for production CSC.

variables.name: Max Matches Per Run
variables.description: Upper bound on matches processed per pass. Lower = more responsive shutdown; higher = catches up faster on first run. On a dedicated server 50–500 is fine.

variables.name: Check Interval (Minutes)
variables.description: How often the worker polls CSC for new matches. 0 = run one pass and exit (don't use this on Pterodactyl, the panel will just keep restarting). >0 = stay alive and sleep this many minutes between passes.
